notes: Multi-agent support: each project now chooses whether it runs Claude Code or OpenAI Codex, with its own executable path, flags and model, in both Windows Terminal tabs and embedded IDE sessions. Adds a terminal font-size stepper to the IDE session rail that applies to every session. Codex specifics: agent paths are auto-detected including npm-global installs, the model field is free text since Codex's lineup moves quickly, and an optional turn-completion callback (off by default) chimes when a turn finishes and reports completion in IDE mode without ever modifying your ~/.codex/config.toml. Note that Codex provides no approval-time event, so Codex sessions never blink for input the way Claude sessions do.
